next_step_text: 75% confidence 6th June- RFI submitted- ck to follow up and meet Mona F2F in HK- need to navigate exec levels asap- well positioned with Mona and high propensity for AI 1st June- RFI finalizing by 2nd June- 21st May- Good update with Mona - RFI is now with us and due by early June.

our_open_deliverables.text: Send follow-up email with clarifying questions on RFI requirements and module details
Provide modular pricing breakdown with indicative pricing and flexible cost model options (source-to-contract vs full S2P)
Deliver detailed presentation on Zycus implementation approach (85-90% fit, configuration, change management) and customer KPIs/outcomes

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the single known active competitor, medium threat. Mona asked direct commercial comparison on 20 May discovery call. Coupa's rigid, partner-only model is misaligned with buyer's stated preferences (modular, flexible, vendor-direct, AI-friendly, configuration over customization). Zycus positioned as the better fit: modular S2P, 85-90% off-the-shelf with configuration flexibility, vendor-direct implementation, agentic AI leadership (buyer impressed at prior event). Incumbent stack (Oracle P2P basic, in-house forms, repository CLM) is being displaced, not defended. No other competitors named. Zycus is well positioned if we reinforce the wedge (modular flexibility, vendor-direct, AI) and map the Economic Buyer before Coupa does.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: How do you compare with your solution with Coupa in terms of commercial? (Mona C Y Chiu, 20 May 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Emphasize modular flexibility (source-to-contract first, expandable), vendor-direct implementation (vs Coupa partner-only), configuration capability (vs Coupa rigid off-the-shelf), and agentic AI leadership (Merlin for guided PR creation, policy compliance automation). Reinforce in RFI response and F2F meeting.

competitors.name: Do Nothing / Incumbent Stack
competitors.quote: Lack of single source of truth for procurement data; dashboards and analysis are difficult to build. No sourcing system in place and outdated supplier onboarding process leads to coverage gaps. Current CLM only serves as a repository. (Mona, 20 May 2026)
competitors.how_we_win: Buyer pain is clear and quantified (disparate systems, poor user experience, compliance risk). RFI submission signals strong intent to move. Risk is low unless budget or exec sponsorship stalls.

champion_strength.source: Contact Role Decision Maker, Avoma discovery call 20 May 2026, Next Step 6 Jun 2026 ('well positioned with Mona', '75% confidence', 'need to navigate exec levels asap')
champion_strength.summary: Developing champion. Mona C Y Chiu (Decision Maker, Procurement Executive) is the single engaged contact, driving the RFI, and well-positioned (75% confidence, impressed by AI demo, clear pain articulation). However, no exec introductions yet, no advocacy evidence beyond RFI submission, and single-threaded. Champion-building (secure her advocacy for exec engagement and process coaching) and multi-threading are the top moves.
champion_strength.champion: Mona C Y Chiu (Decision Maker, Procurement Executive)

sources: Next Step 6 Jun 2026: 'need to navigate exec levels asap'
Avoma discovery call 20 May 2026: no budget/commercial authority discussion
Account-level contacts: David Fan (CFO), Jacqueline Lam Chu Wan (Head of Strategic Procurement) — not yet engaged on this opp
meddpicc_economic_buyer.narrative: Economic Buyer is not yet identified. No buyer participant on the 20 May discovery call discussed budget authority, commercial approval, or CFO/C-level sign-off. The Decision Maker (Mona C Y Chiu, Procurement Executive) is driving the RFI and evaluation but has not indicated she controls the commercial decision. The Next Step (6 Jun) flags 'need to navigate exec levels asap', signaling that exec sponsorship is recognized as a gap by the deal owner. Account-level contacts include the CFO (David Fan, Executive Director of Finance, Procurement and Investment Office) and Head of Strategic Procurement (Jacqueline Lam Chu Wan), both plausible Economic Buyer candidates, but neither has been engaged on this opportunity. The 20 May call included no pricing or budget discussion beyond modular flexibility and a comparison question about Coupa commercials. Mapping the Economic Buyer (likely CFO or Head of Strategic Procurement) and securing their active sponsorship is the highest-leverage move.

recommended_moves: Map the Economic Buyer within 7 days via F2F meeting with Decision Maker in Hong Kong.
Deliver RFI response emphasizing modular pricing, vendor-direct implementation, and Merlin agentic AI centerpiece.
Secure Decision Maker introduction to CFO and Head of Strategic Procurement within 14 days.
